allowed_use: Use as corporate procurement, restart, or future-development evidence with dates and party names.
blocked_use: Do not write planned or contracted nuclear power as delivered, additional, cheap, safe, carbon-free in hourly operation, or physically serving a specific data center unless shown.
required_evidence_before_final: Official contract/press source plus regulatory, operational, and delivery-status evidence for any stronger claim.

allowed_use: Use as a speed-to-power or backup/on-site generation response only when permit/operator evidence exists.
blocked_use: Do not infer turbine count, MW, runtime, emissions, legal status, fuel mix, or health impact from imagery or secondary summaries.
required_evidence_before_final: Primary permit, environmental filing, utility document, or operator statement with dates.

allowed_use: Use as an infrastructure-response category in DOE/IEA planning.
blocked_use: Do not claim batteries solve AI power demand, eliminate grid upgrades, reduce emissions, or serve a named campus without matching evidence.
required_evidence_before_final: Project-specific interconnection, capacity, duration, dispatch, and site evidence.

allowed_use: Use to show queue/lead-time/planning friction and physical grid texture.
blocked_use: Do not claim named project delays, complete queue causality, utility-wide impacts, or capacity values without same-scope source.
required_evidence_before_final: Utility filing, interconnection record, DOE/EPRI report row, or regulator document.

allowed_use: Use PPAs/certificates/procurement claims as contractual or accounting signals.
blocked_use: Do not equate procurement with delivered electrons, hourly fuel mix, marginal generation, or actual data-center carbon intensity.
required_evidence_before_final: Hourly matching data, grid-region supply mix, contract details, and emissions/accounting method.

allowed_use: Use power visuals to explain infrastructure pressure, not to assign precise energy to a token, model, user request, or company product.
blocked_use: Do not state energy-per-token, query energy, training energy, product fuel mix, or customer workload from facility/power sources alone.
required_evidence_before_final: Model/workload measurement, hardware utilization, PUE/cooling, region/time grid data, and methodology.
